revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Benkei-ga-ana Kofun]][[ja:弁慶ヶ穴古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Benkei-ga-ana Kofun | native_name =弁慶ヶ穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Benkei-ga-ana Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.026|N|130.685861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|33.6|N|130|41|9.1|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Benkei-ga-ana Kofun'''|弁慶ヶ穴古墳|qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Kumairi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192112|title=弁慶ヶ穴古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Benkei-ga-ana Kofun is located is located at the western end of the Kumai-ri Plateau, about 1.2 kilometers north of the urban center of Yamaga City, next to the Yamaga City Yahata Elementary School. It is thought to be a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, built at the end of the late Kofun period; however, the anterior part of the tomb facing south west has almost disappeared, leaving only the posterior circular portion with a diameter of 15 meters and a height of 5.7 meters. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is mad of huge stones of {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}, and consists of a horizontal passageway, an anterior chamber, and a posterior chamber. There is a relief of a human figure at the entrance to the burial chamber, and on the walls of the antechamber are geometric patterns that combine circles, diamonds, and triangles, as well as pictures of five horses and people, a boat with horses, birds, cargo and boats, and equestrian figures. The "cargo" in the boats is believed to be coffins, which has supported a theory that {{ill|ship burial|en|ship burial|qid=Q1145397|lt=boat burials|1=Ship burial}} were practiced in Kofun period Japan, remnants of which has survived in the belief that the afterlife is beyond the sea. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> Currently, viewing is strictly limited due to the severe fading of the colors, but a life-size model is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Tomb Museum. [[Archaeological excavation|Excavated artifacts]] include ''[[:en:Tsuba|Tsuba]]'' (sword guards), beads, iron arrowheads, and gold rings.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://kumamoto.guide/spots/detail/11141 Kumamoto Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.yamaga.kumamoto.jp/kiji00392/index.html Yamaga city home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://kofunkan.pref.kumamoto.jp Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofun Museum] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
Benkei-ga-ana Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
